article_title: Obligations of providers of GPAI models with systemic risk
obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk shall keep track of, document, and report without undue delay to the AI Office and, as appropriate, to national competent authorities, relevant information about serious incidents and possible corrective measures to address them.
scoring_text: Skill in AI serious incident management and regulatory reporting, including incident tracking systems, documentation procedures, AI Office and national authority reporting protocols, and corrective measure identification and reporting.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI serious incident management and regulatory reporting, including incident tracking systems, documentation procedures, AI Office and national authority reporting protocols, and corrective measure identification and reporting.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 55(1)(c) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.650, cosine: 0.718). Secondary: RC-S-008 (RC, cosine: 0.651), AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.650).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.5/10 (raw 0.650). Shared vocabulary: [ai, documentation, identification, skill]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [authority, corrective, incident, management, measure, national] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, evaluation, harm] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.882 (P=0.875, R=0.890). Balanced P/R — the 28-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.4%). Semantic tension between "Skill in AI serious incident management and regulatory reporting, including inci" and "Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identifi". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.718 > STS 0.650 (gap +0.068). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, documentation, identification, skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 21.2%. Shared tokens: [ai, documentation, identification, skill]. EU AI Act-only: [authority, corrective, incident, management, measure, national]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, evaluation, harm].
